insights.claim: The population-weighted U.S. average for high bp is 34.41%.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted mean across ZCTAs with estimates.

insights.claim: The most-deprived ADI tenth averages 15.8 points higher than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted ADI national-rank decile means; ecological, not causal.

insights.claim: Across ZCTAs, high bp is most associated with area deprivation index rank (Spearman rho=0.679, n=32,191).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation; place-level association only.
Maximum ZCTA value; ties broken by population.

insights.claim: About 13.9 million people live in ZCTAs at or above the high-burden threshold (45.8%).
insights.method_note: Population in ZCTAs at or above the 90th percentile (45.8%).
